perturbation.original_text: Prices for long distance termination shall be as posted on the Partner Portal and Telnet understands and agrees that, other than for those destinations noted in Schedule A as being fixed in price for the term of this Agreement, such prices are variable and subject to change without notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Prices for long distance termination shall be as posted on the Partner Portal and Telnet understands and agrees that prices are variable and subject to change with notice.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'other than for those destinations noted in Schedule A as being fixed in price for the term of this Agreement,' contradicts Schedule A where specific pricing IS fixed. The edited text states that ALL prices are variable and subject to change which goes against the schedule A agreement.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that prices for some destinations in Schedule A were fixed. The changed text removes this exception, stating that ALL prices are variable and subject to change with notice. This directly contradicts the pricing listed in Schedule A, which includes fixed prices for certain services, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction. The altered text makes the terms regarding pricing unenforceable since Schedule A clearly states some prices are fixed and the changed text indicates the opposite.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Ownership and Privacy of Records Galaxy shall own the Records, but it shall use the information contained therein only as allowed by the terms and conditions and intent of this Agreement. Galaxy shall treat the Records as Confidential Information and it shall not provide to or allow access by any third party except as required or allowed by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Ownership and Privacy of Records Galaxy shall own the Records. Galaxy shall treat the Records as Confidential Information and it shall not provide to or allow access by any third party except as required or allowed by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting ', but it shall use the information contained therein only as allowed by the terms and conditions and intent of this Agreement.', Galaxy is given unrestricted ownership of the Records. This contradicts section 10.5. Subscribers where Galaxy has the right to contact Subscribers directly and solicit such Subscribers to become subscribers of Galaxy, an affiliate thereof or of another client of Galaxy's, meaning they can use the records for marketing purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.5 Subscribers If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to section 10.2, Galaxy shall have the right to contact Subscribers directly and solicit such Subscribers to become subscribers of Galaxy, an affiliate thereof or of another client of Galaxy's.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited Galaxy's usage of the Records. The changed text removes this limitation, granting Galaxy unrestricted ownership and usage rights. This directly contradicts section 10.5, which grants Galaxy the right to contact subscribers for marketing purposes only upon termination of the agreement. Now, under the modified 4.2, Galaxy could argue it has the right to use the records for marketing at any time, not just upon termination. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the scope of permitted use of the Records is altered, conflicting with the explicitly stated conditions in section 10.5.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Termination for Default Subject to section 13.2, either Party may terminate this Agreement, effective immediately, if the other commits a material breach of it, commits any material fraudulent act in performing any of its obligations or makes any material misrepresentation to the other or commits an act of malfeasance or misfeasance in the performance of its or his duties or is unable or unwilling to perform its obligations and duties under this Agreement which circumstances will include, but not be limited to: If a receiver, trustee in bankruptcy or similar officer is appointed to take charge of any of its assets; or If it files for relief under any applicable bankruptcy laws.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Termination for Default Either Party may terminate this Agreement, effective immediately, if the other commits a material breach of it, commits any material fraudulent act in performing any of its obligations or makes any material misrepresentation to the other or commits an act of malfeasance or misfeasance in the performance of its or his duties or is unable or unwilling to perform its obligations and duties under this Agreement which circumstances will include, but not be limited to: If a receiver, trustee in bankruptcy or similar officer is appointed to take charge of any of its assets; or If it files for relief under any applicable bankruptcy laws.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Subject to section 13.2', the termination clause now lacks the condition of the 'Grace Period' section. This omission creates contradiction with clause 13.2 as it removes the requirement for a grace period before termination, potentially allowing immediate termination for breaches that could be rectified within the grace period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2 Default If either of the Parties should be in default (the "Defaulting Party") of any obligation or requirement under this Agreement, the Party affected may give written notice to the Defaulting Party specifying the default and will give the Defaulting Party a grace period of 30 days (the "Grace Period") to cure such default or to take such reasonable steps to cure without undue delay such default prior to seeking any remedy it may have on account of such default. The Defaulting Party shall lose no rights under this Agreement if it cures the stated default within the Grace Period.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'Subject to section 13.2' in clause 10.2 directly contradicts clause 13.2, which provides a 30-day grace period for curing defaults. By removing this condition, section 10.2 now allows for immediate termination upon a material breach, conflicting with the grace period stipulated in section 13.2. The original text allowed for 30 day cure period, change text removed that ability, causing direct contradiction.
